heading: Modification of goals; additional rulemaking authority
label: 13254 Modification of goals; additional rulemaking authority
notes: References in TextThis Act, referred to in subsec. (c), is , , , known as the Energy Policy Act of 1992. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Short Title note set out under and Tables.Pub. L. 102–486106 Stat. 2776section 13201 of this titleOct. 24, 1992

children.heading: Examination of goals
children.label: a Examination of goals
children.text: Within 3 years after , and periodically thereafter, the Secretary shall examine the goals established under , in the context of the program goals stated under , to determine if the goals under , including the applicable percentage requirements and dates, should be modified under this section. The Secretary shall publish in the Federal Register the results of each examination under this subsection and provide an opportunity for public comment.October 24, 1992section 13252(b)(2) of this titlesection 13252(a) of this titlesection 13252(b)(2) of this title

children.heading: Modification of goals
children.label: b Modification of goals
children.text: If, after analysis of information obtained in connection with carrying out subsection (a) or , or other information, and taking into account the determination of technical and economic feasibility made under , the Secretary determines that goals described in , including the percentage requirements or dates, are not achievable, the Secretary, in consultation with appropriate Federal agencies, shall, by rule, establish goals that are achievable, for purposes of this subchapter. The modification of goals under this section may include changing the target dates specified in .section 13252 of this titlesection 13252(b)(2) of this titlesection 13252(b)(2) of this titlesection 13252(b)(2) of this title

children.heading: Additional rulemaking authority
children.label: c Additional rulemaking authority
children.text: If the Secretary determines that the achievement of goals described in would result in a significant and correctable failure to meet the program goals described in , the Secretary shall issue such additional regulations as are necessary to remedy such failure. The Secretary shall have no authority under this Act to mandate the production of alternative fueled vehicles or to specify, as applicable, the models, lines, or types of, or marketing or pricing practices, policies, or strategies for, vehicles subject to this Act. Nothing in this Act shall be construed to give the Secretary authority to mandate marketing or pricing practices, policies, or strategies for alternative fuels or to mandate the production or delivery of such fuels.section 13252(b)(2) of this titlesection 13252(a) of this title
